remediation_commit_note: This value is intentionally null: the commit that includes this file cannot contain its own resulting hash. Resolve via 'git rev-parse HEAD' immediately after the remediation commit described in CLAUDE_REMEDIATION_IMPLEMENTATION_REPORT.md is created.

findings.original_finding: render-docx --output equal to --input silently overwrote the input file with binary DOCX content, exit code 0.
findings.root_cause: _safe_output_path only confined the resolved path to cwd and blocked reserved device names; it never compared resolved output to resolved input.
findings.fix: Added paths.is_same_file_target() (resolved-path equality + os.path.samefile), wired into render-docx, validate --output, finalize-word, and build-artifact's derived docx path.

findings.original_finding: Footnote-free documents always failed structural validation because separator/continuation footnotes never carry w:bidi and the check was unconditional.
findings.root_cause: _footnote_findings required w:bidi anywhere in footnotes.xml, not scoped to real (non-reserved-ID) footnotes.
findings.fix: RTL check now scoped to non-reserved footnote IDs only; also added detection of footnote references with a missing footnotes.xml part (footnotes_part_missing), a related gap found during remediation.

findings.original_finding: If DispatchEx never returns, word_pid is never captured, so timeout cleanup cannot identify/terminate the orphaned WINWORD.EXE process.
findings.root_cause: Pre-dispatch process snapshot was only held in worker memory, never persisted, so it was lost if the worker was killed mid-hang.
findings.fix: Pre-dispatch winword.exe PID snapshot and worker start time are now persisted to diagnostics before DispatchEx is called. On timeout without a captured word_pid, the parent diffs a fresh snapshot and only terminates a single, executable-verified, time-plausible new process. Multiple simultaneous candidates are left running rather than guessed at.
findings.residual_risk: If multiple new winword.exe processes appear in the same narrow window, none are auto-terminated (ownership cannot be disambiguated); this is a deliberate safety trade-off, not a bug.

findings.original_finding: loader.load_state()'s dedicated schema_version error path is dead code; the real pipeline relies solely on the generic JSON Schema const check.
findings.root_cause: The manual version comparison in load_state() was unreachable: research_state_errors() already raises for any schema violation, including a bad schema_version, before that line executes.
findings.fix: Removed the dead, provably-unreachable manual check; load_state() now relies exclusively on the schema-based check, matching the pipeline's already-tested approach. Removed the now-fully-unused SUPPORTED_RESEARCH_SCHEMA_VERSION constant.

findings.original_finding: Implicit schema-directory override activates purely on path existence, with no integrity check, in an installed-package layout where the ancestor path is unrelated to the project.
findings.root_cause: SCHEMAS_DIR selection walked two parents above the package looking for a schemas/ directory with no verification the ancestor was actually this project's checkout.
findings.fix: resolve_schemas_dir() now defaults to the packaged schema directory unconditionally; overriding requires an explicit LEGAL_RESEARCH_SKILL_USE_WORKTREE_SCHEMAS=1 environment variable plus a pyproject.toml sanity check on the candidate root.

findings.original_finding: generated_at/created_at defaults to a hardcoded literal presented as if it were a real generation timestamp.
findings.root_cause: RenderConfig.created_at's fixed default was undocumented as such.
findings.fix: Documentation-only: named the constant DEFAULT_BUILD_EPOCH and added docstrings/comments clarifying it is a fixed build epoch, not a real timestamp, at every point it is consumed. A behavioral fix (real-time default) was attempted and reverted because it broke the project's deliberately-tested byte-reproducibility guarantee across independent CLI invocations.
findings.residual_risk: The value still defaults to a fixed epoch rather than real time in production; this is intentional (reproducibility over real-time accuracy) and now clearly documented rather than silently misleading.

findings.original_finding: Zip-bomb compression-ratio heuristic false-positives on legitimate long, highly-repetitive text.
findings.root_cause: Pure ratio threshold with no minimum size floor.
findings.fix: Combined ratio threshold with a compressed-size floor before evaluating ratio, plus independent per-part and total-uncompressed-size caps.

findings.original_finding: No CI workflow builds or publishes release assets; the published v0.3.0 wheel has no CI-verifiable provenance.
findings.root_cause: ci.yml only triggers on push to main / PR; no tag-triggered or publishing workflow existed.
findings.fix: Added a tag-triggered workflow that builds, tests, hashes, and uploads the wheel as a CI artifact. Does not auto-create a GitHub Release or publish to PyPI, per this task's constraints.
findings.residual_risk: Does not retroactively provide provenance for the already-published v0.3.0 asset; only benefits future tags.

findings.original_finding: Wheel builds are not byte-reproducible across checkout environments (CRLF/LF variance, no .gitattributes).
findings.root_cause: core.autocrlf=true with no .gitattributes made checkout line-ending behavior environment-dependent.
findings.fix: Added .gitattributes pinning tracked text files to eol=lf. While verifying this, found and fixed a live CRLF-on-disk inconsistency in one fixture (working tree bytes differed from the committed LF blob but were masked as 'clean' by autocrlf-aware git status); corrected to match the committed blob exactly (verified via git hash-object equality -- zero content change).
findings.residual_risk: Cannot retroactively make the already-published v0.3.0 wheel byte-reproducible; only prevents recurrence for future tags.

findings.original_finding: No output file-extension validation on render-docx.
findings.root_cause: No suffix check existed on the render-docx output path.
findings.fix: Added case-insensitive .docx extension requirement and a directory-vs-file guard to render-docx and finalize-word output paths.

findings.original_finding: GitHub Actions pinned to mutable version tags, not commit SHAs.
findings.root_cause: actions/checkout@v4 and actions/setup-python@v5 used tag references.
findings.fix: Pinned both actions to commit SHAs resolved via 'gh api repos/actions/<name>/git/refs/tags/<tag>', with version comments retained for readability.

findings.original_finding: Runtime/dev dependencies had version floors but no ceilings.
findings.fix: Added major-version ceilings (jsonschema<5, pytest<10, pytest-cov<8, ruff<1) above the currently-installed versions.

findings.original_finding: Missing required dependency causes a raw traceback instead of a clean CLI error.
findings.root_cause: legal_research_skill.cli (and its jsonschema dependency) was imported unconditionally at the top of __main__.py, before any error handling existed.
findings.fix: Deferred and guarded the import inside a new __main__.run() function; retargeted both python -m and the console-script entry point to it.

findings.original_finding: No LICENSE file in a public repository.
findings.root_cause: No license has ever been added to the repository.
findings.fix: Strengthened the existing disclosure with an explicit statement that no reuse rights are granted by default without a LICENSE file. No LICENSE was created, per this task's explicit instruction not to choose a license on the owner's behalf.
findings.residual_risk: The underlying gap remains: the project has no license and therefore grants no reuse rights by default.

findings.original_finding: Broad except Exception usage in word/ COM call sites, defensible but under-documented.
findings.root_cause: N/A (documentation gap, not a functional defect).
findings.fix: Added an explanatory comment to the two ScreenUpdating/AutomationSecurity best-effort suppress(Exception) sites clarifying the intentional non-fatal semantics.
findings.residual_risk: None; remaining except Exception sites were already judged structurally necessary in the original audit and were left unchanged.

remaining_risks: WORD-1: ambiguous multi-process detection intentionally leaves orphan(s) running rather than guessing ownership.
REL-1/REL-2: cannot retroactively provide provenance or byte-reproducibility for the already-published v0.3.0 asset; only future tags benefit.
LIC-1: no LICENSE file exists; project remains all-rights-reserved by default, unresolved by design in this task.
verdict: POST-REMEDIATION AUDIT: PASS WITH LIMITATIONS -- PROJECT READY FOR v0.3.1 WITH DOCUMENTED WORD LIMITATION
